narration_text: Now we'll see how coaxial cables work, notice that they have two concentric copper conductors, allowing for bidirectional and broadband communication. Look at the image on the slide, observe how the inner and outer conductors are arranged, this design enables multiple frequency channels to coexist on the same cable, achieving speeds of hundreds of megabits per second per channel. I want you to focus on the diagram, see how this setup enables high-speed point-to-point transmission with low error rates. Let's think about this together, the use of repeaters and the cable's design make it immune to electromagnetic noise, now this understanding of coaxial cables will help us transition to the next topic, exploring even faster transmission methods.
